hawkish_phrases: an ongoing tightening of monetary policy over the forecast period would be appropriate
a small margin of excess demand still emerges by early 2020
Wage growth and domestic cost pressures are firming gradually
The prospect of excess demand over the forecast period has reduced the degree to which it is appropriate for the MPC to accommodate an extended period of inflation above the target
the UK economy has a very limited degree of slack
dovish_phrases: For the majority of members, an increase in Bank Rate was not required at this meeting
any future increases in Bank Rate are likely to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
GDP growth in the first quarter was 0.1%, 0.3 percentage points lower than expected
business investment is still restrained by Brexit-related uncertainties
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.5%, with the majority stating that ongoing tightening would be appropriate over the forecast period to address emerging excess demand, though an increase was not required at this meeting.
